title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake
description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges, tender interior, and generous medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Each bite offers a delightful contrast of textures and a rich umami flavor, making it a perfect appetizer or a satisfying meal, especially on a rainy day.

ingredients.notes: For extra crispiness, optional but recommended
White and light green parts, cut into 3-4 inch lengths
Peeled, deveined, patted dry, cut into bite-sized pieces
Cleaned, scored, patted dry, cut into bite-sized pieces
Thinly sliced diagonally, for garnish/slight heat, optional

instructions.title: Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and prepare all seafood. For shrimp, peel, devein, and pat dry before cutting into bite-sized pieces. For squid, clean thoroughly, score lightly, and cut into rings or bite-sized pieces, then pat dry. If using mussels or clams, ensure they are scrubbed clean and shelled. Slice the yellow onion thinly and mince the garlic. Wash the green onions well, trim the ends, and cut them into 3-4 inch lengths. If using red chili, slice it thinly on the diagonal. Set aside.

instructions.title: Whisk the Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, Korean rice flour (if using), salt, and baking powder (if using). In a separate bowl, whisk the cold water (or anchovy broth) and the large egg until well combined. Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking continuously until a smooth batter forms. Be careful not to overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to pancake batter.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce (Cho-Ganjang)
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine all the dipping sauce ingredients: soy sauce, rice vinegar, water, sugar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, and minced garlic. Stir well until the sugar dissolves. If desired, add a pinch of red chili flakes for a subtle kick. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. Set aside.

instructions.title: Assemble and Fry the First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large, non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add about 2-3 tablespoons of cooking oil, ensuring the entire surface is coated. Once the oil is hot, pour about 1/4 to 1/3 of the batter into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin, round pancake. Immediately arrange a generous layer of green onion pieces, a portion of the seafood mix (shrimp, squid, mussels), sliced yellow onion, and minced garlic evenly over the batter.

instructions.title: Cook Until Golden and Crispy
instructions.text: Press the toppings gently into the batter with a spatula. Cook for about 3-4 minutes on the first side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy and the batter is mostly set. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges if needed to promote crispiness. Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Cook for another 3-4 minutes on the second side until golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. The exact cooking time may vary based on your stove and pan.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Once cooked, slide the Haemul Pajeon onto a cutting board and cut it into wedges or squares. Serve immediately while hot, accompanied by the prepared Cho-Ganjang dipping sauce. Repeat the process for the remaining batter and ingredients, adding more oil to the pan as needed for each pancake. Enjoy the authentic taste of Korean seafood pancake!

tips: **Use Cold Water:** Using very cold water (or even sparkling water) for the batter helps create a crispier pancake.
**Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** Cook one pancake at a time to ensure even cooking and optimal crispiness.
**Generous Oil is Key:** Don't be shy with oil, especially when flipping. It helps achieve that desirable crunchy exterior.
**Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from seafood can make the pancake soggy. Ensure all seafood is thoroughly patted dry before adding to the batter.
**Serve Hot:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan while it's still hot and crispy.
article:
## History of Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

Haemul Pajeon, a savory pancake brimming with seafood and green onions, holds a cherished place in Korean culinary tradition. Its origins can be traced back to the Joseon Dynasty, where variations of *jeon* (Korean pancakes) were enjoyed by all social classes. Pajeon, specifically, emphasizes *pa* (green onions) as its primary vegetable. The addition of *haemul* (seafood) elevated it to a more celebratory dish, often enjoyed on special occasions or as a hearty accompaniment to makgeolli (Korean rice wine), particularly on rainy days when the sizzling sound of the pancake on the griddle is said to evoke the comforting patter of raindrops. It reflects Korea's abundant coastline and the creative use of fresh local ingredients.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Haemul Pajeon achieves its authentic appeal through a careful balance of ingredients and techniques. The combination of all-purpose flour and Korean rice flour in the batter is crucial; the rice flour contributes to an exceptional crispiness that standard flour alone cannot provide, while the all-purpose flour offers structure. Using cold water helps prevent gluten development, resulting in a tender interior. The generous amount of fresh green onions provides a refreshing bite and aromatic depth, perfectly complementing the sweet and savory notes of the seafood. Frying in sufficient oil at the right temperature ensures a beautifully golden-brown, crunchy crust, contrasting wonderfully with the soft, flavorful filling.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

To master Haemul Pajeon, focus on a few key details. Firstly, ensure your green onions are fresh and vibrant; they are the heart of the dish. Secondly, don't overmix the batter; a slightly lumpy batter is fine and helps avoid toughness. The secret to a truly crispy pajeon lies in the heat of your pan and the amount of oil. Get your pan hot before adding oil, and use enough oil to almost shallow-fry the pancake, especially around the edges. When flipping, be confident and use a sturdy, wide spatula. Lastly, for the most authentic flavor, consider using anchovy broth instead of plain water for the batter, as it adds a subtle layer of umami that enhances the seafood.

## Serving Suggestions

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile. It shines as a standalone appetizer, especially when shared amongst friends over a glass of chilled makgeolli or soju. It also makes a delightful side dish (banchan) in a larger Korean meal, complementing stews like Kimchi Jjigae or soups. For a complete experience, always serve it immediately after frying, while it's still warm and crispy, alongside the tangy and savory Cho-Ganjang dipping sauce. You can also customize the dipping sauce with a touch of gochugaru (Korean chili powder) for a spicy kick, or finely chopped fresh chili for a burst of heat. For an extra touch of elegance, garnish with a few extra toasted sesame seeds or a sprig of fresh cilantro.

jsonLd.name: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake
jsonLd.description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges, tender interior, and generous medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Each bite offers a delightful contrast of textures and a rich umami flavor, making it a perfect appetizer or a satisfying meal, especially on a rainy day.
jsonLd.keywords: Korean, Korean, authentic, traditional, seafood, Korean, authentic, traditional, seafood, pancake, Haemul Pajeon, savory, comfort food
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Korean rice flour (Ssalgaru) (For extra crispiness, optional but recommended)
1/2 teaspoon Baking powder (Optional, for a lighter texture)
1 bunch (about 8-10 stalks) Large green onions (scallions) (White and light green parts, cut into 3-4 inch lengths)
8 oz Shrimp (Peeled, deveined, patted dry, cut into bite-sized pieces)
8 oz Squid (Cleaned, scored, patted dry, cut into bite-sized pieces)
1 Red chili (Hong-gochu) (Thinly sliced diagonally, for garnish/slight heat, optional)
1/2 cup Cooking oil (e.g., vegetable or canola) (For frying)
1 teaspoon Toasted sesame seeds (For dipping sauce)
Optional, for dipping sauce Pinch of red chili flakes (Gochugaru)

For squid, clean thoroughly, score lightly, and cut into rings or bite-sized pieces, then pat dry. If using mussels or clams, ensure they are scrubbed clean and shelled. Slice the yellow onion thinly and mince the garlic. Wash the green onions well, trim the ends, and cut them into 3-4 inch lengths. If using red chili, slice it thinly on the diagonal. Set aside.

Immediately arrange a generous layer of green onion pieces, a portion of the seafood mix (shrimp, squid, mussels), sliced yellow onion, and minced garlic evenly over the batter.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook Until Golden and Crispy
recipeInstructions.text: Press the toppings gently into the batter with a spatula. Cook for about 3-4 minutes on the first side, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy and the batter is mostly set. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges if needed to promote crispiness. Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Cook for another 3-4 minutes on the second side until golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. The exact cooking time may vary based on your stove and pan.

recipeInstructions.name: Serve Immediately
recipeInstructions.text: Once cooked, slide the Haemul Pajeon onto a cutting board and cut it into wedges or squares. Serve immediately while hot, accompanied by the prepared Cho-Ganjang dipping sauce. Repeat the process for the remaining batter and ingredients, adding more oil to the pan as needed for each pancake. Enjoy the authentic taste of Korean seafood pancake!
